page: Microsoft Azure L64s_v4 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the Lsv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 vCPUs mapped to 32 physical Intel Xeon 8573C cores with hyper-threading enabled. The dedicated CPU operates at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz and includes a 260 MB L3 cache. The hardware configuration is equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, providing 8.0 GB of memory per physical core. It includes 15,359 GB of local storage and does not bundle any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the L64s_v4 demonstrates strong multi-core and memory performance. The Geekbench multi-core test places the instance in the top tier with a score of 21,563.0, complemented by top-tier image processing performance and static web serving throughput of 93.83 GB/sec. Multi-core stress-ng CPU performance and large-block memory bandwidth read/write speeds are categorized as strong. However, single-core performance shows moderate relative results, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and single-threaded bzip3 decompression falling into the average category.

The instance offers high resource density, combining a large local storage capacity of over 15 TB with a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. This profile provides qualitative cost efficiency for data-intensive workloads that require massive local storage and high memory capacity without the added expense of discrete GPU hardware. Given the strong database operations benchmark and high Redis SET throughput of 78.56 million operations per second, this server is optimized for large-scale databases, high-throughput web serving, and memory-intensive data processing.
description: Microsoft Azure L64s_v4 is an x86_64 server instance in the Lsv4 family, powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 64 vCPUs and 512.0 GB of RAM. It features 15,359 GB of local storage and operates on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor without bundled GPUs. The CPU includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and AMX. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core performance, image processing, and static web serving throughput of 93.83 GB/sec, alongside strong database operations and memory bandwidth. However, single-core performance remains average. This high-density storage and memory configuration is suited for large-scale databases, high-throughput web serving, and data-intensive processing workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L64s_v4 is an x86_64 server featuring 64 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 15,359 GB of local storage. It delivers top-tier multi-core and web serving performance for database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L64s_v4 is an x86_64 server with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 15,359 GB of local storage, optimized for large-scale database workloads.
tagline: A high-density storage and memory-optimized server featuring sixty-four virtual cores and over fifteen terabytes of local storage for database workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 32 physical Intel Xeon 8573C cores running at 4.2 GHz
512 GB of system memory paired with 15,359 GB of local storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU and static web serving benchmark results
Average single-core performance relative to multi-core capabilities
Optimized for large-scale databases, high-throughput web serving, and data processing
